prose.en: Prohibit the direct connection of {param:sc-07.25_odp.01} to an external network without the use of {param:sc-07.25_odp.02}.

guidance.en: A direct connection is a dedicated physical or virtual connection between two or more systems. Organizations typically do not have complete control over external networks, including the Internet. Boundary protection devices (e.g., firewalls, gateways, and routers) mediate communications and information flows between unclassified national security systems and external networks.

prose.en: the direct connection of {param:sc-07.25_odp.01} to an external network without the use of {param:sc-07.25_odp.02} is prohibited.

objects.en: system configuration settings and associated documentation
organizational personnel with information security responsibilities
organizational personnel with boundary protection responsibilities
Mechanisms prohibiting the direct connection of unclassified national security systems to an external network

guidelines.en: the unclassified national security system prohibited from directly connecting to an external network is defined;
the boundary protection device required for a direct connection to an external network is defined;
